class Solution {

public:

    string reverseWords(string s) {

        vector<string> words;

        string currentWord;

        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); ++i) {

            char c = s[i];

            if (c != ' ') {

                currentWord += c;

            } else {

                if (!currentWord.empty()) {

                    words.push_back(currentWord);

                    currentWord.clear();

                }

            }

        }

        if (!currentWord.empty()) {

            words.push_back(currentWord);

        }

        // Reverse the words

        reverse(words.begin(), words.end());

        // Now build the result

        string result;

        if (words.empty()) return result; // Shouldn't happen per constraints

        result = words[0];

        for (int i = 1; i < words.size(); ++i) {

            result += ' ' + words[i];

        }

        return result;

    }

};

solution: 
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

class Solution {
public:
    string reverseWords(string s) {
        vector<string> words;
        string currentWord;
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); ++i) {
            char c = s[i];
            if (c != ' ') {
                currentWord += c;
            } else {
                if (!currentWord.empty()) {
                    words.push_back(currentWord);
                    currentWord.clear();
                }
            }
        }
        if (!currentWord.empty()) {
            words.push_back(currentWord);
        }
        reverse(words.begin(), words.end());
        string result;
        if (words.empty()) return result;
        result = words[0];
        for (int i = 1; i < words.size(); ++i) {
            result += ' ' + words[i];
        }
        return result;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution splits the input string into words by ignoring multiple spaces, then reverses the list of words, and finally joins them with single spaces. Key strengths include handling all edge cases (leading/trailing spaces, multiple spaces) efficiently. The main weakness is O(n) space due to storing words in a vector.
